zh.lifeSummary: 當Adelicia de Verdun於1065年出生在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾時，她的父親Bertram I de Verdun26歲，母親Maude de Modena16歲。她和Robert Peverell至少有1個兒子和4個女兒。她去世於1132年在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾卡可頓享年67歲，埋葬在英國坎布利其爾坎布利其。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Verdun
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from any of several places in France called Verdun. The placename is probably of Gaulish origin, deriving from ver(n) ‘alder’ + dūn ‘hill, fortress’.
Spanish and Catalan: habitational name from Verdú in Catalonia (Spain), which has the same origin as the French name (see 1 above).
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Verdon  , a cognate of 1 above. This surname is very rare in Britain.

variants.name: Virden
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Verdun  .

variants.name: Verdon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Marcel, Pierre, Renaud, Sylvie.
French: habitational name from (Le) Verdon, the name of several places in various parts of France, for example in Dordogne, Gironde, and Marne. Compare   Ferdon  .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Verdun, the name of a fief in Vessey (Manche), France. Compare   Verdun  .

nameMeanings.name: Adelicia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Feminine form of the Late Latin name Delicius, a derivative of deliciae ‘delight’. Use as a given name seems to be a modern phenomenon; it is not found in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Delysia
meanings: Feminine form of the Late Latin name Delicius, a derivative of deliciae ‘delight’. Use as a given name seems to be a modern phenomenon; it is not found in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Adelicia de Verdun was born in 1065, in Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Bertram I de Verdun, was 26 and her mother, Maude de Modena, was 16. She had at least 1 son and 4 daughters with Robert Peverell. She died in 1132, in Caxton, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 67, and was buried in Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Adelicia de Verdun est née en 1065, à Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Bertram I de Verdun, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Maude de Modena, avait 16 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles avec Robert Peverell. Elle est décédée en 1132, à Caxton, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterrée à Canteburge, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre.

de.Farnham Royal, South Buckingham, Bucking: Farnham Royal, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Adelicia de Verdun 1065 in Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Bertram I de Verdun 26 und ihre Mutter Maude de Modena 16. Sie hatte mit Robert Peverell mindestens 1 Sohn und 4 Töchter. Sie starb 1132 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Caxton, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, England begraben.

Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio e 4 figlie con Robert Peverell. Morì nel 1132, nel Caxton, Cambridgeshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolta a Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Adelicia de Verdunが1065年にイギリスイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアで生まれた時，父Bertram I de Verdunは26歳，母Maude de Modenaは16歳であった。彼女はRobert Peverellとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1132年にイギリスイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアカクストンで67歳で死去し，イギリスカンブリジェシュアイアカンブリッジにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Adelicia de Verdun(이)가 1065년 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Bertram I de Verdun(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maude de Modena(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 Robert Peverell(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1132년 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 카스턴에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 캄브리지실르 캄브리지에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Adelicia de Verdun nasceu em 1065, em Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Bertram I de Verdun, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Maude de Modena, tinha 16 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho e 4 filhas com Robert Peverell. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Adelicia de Verdun родилась в 1065 г. в местности камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Bertram I de Verdun было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Maude de Modena, 16. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери от Robert Peverell. Она умерла в 1132 г. в местности какстон, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 67 лет и была похоронена в местности камбридж, камбриджешир, Англия.

Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 4 hijas con Robert Peverell. Murió en 1132, en Caxton, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultada en Cantebriche, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra.
